ssh -i C:\Users\aleay\.ssh\id_rsa root@kubernetes.polito.it -p 44404

wg-quick up wg0

QBS8vPbo9lNAiexDGLVU+0b/34f+Qet4gGsyzzIpplI=
51820

# /etc/wireguard/wg0.conf
[Interface]
 
# The IP address of this host in the wireguard tunnels
Address = 10.0.0.1
 
# Every Raspberry Pi connects via UDP to this port. Your Cloud VM must be reachable on this port via UDP from the internet.
ListenPort = 51820
 
# Set the private key to the value of the privatekey file generated by the previous command
PrivateKey = QBS8vPbo9lNAiexDGLVU+0b/34f+Qet4gGsyzzIpplI=


wg set wg0 peer FG9df3hvunGToUE/gpjQgAMlRxq36LlS2ujzFLkgmCA= allowed-ips 10.0.0.2/32

PostUp   = iptables -A FORWARD -i %i -j ACCEPT; iptables -A FORWARD -o %i -j ACCEPT; iptables -t nat -A POSTROUTING -o ens2 -j MASQUERADE
PostDown = iptables -D FORWARD -i %i -j ACCEPT; iptables -D FORWARD -o %i -j ACCEPT; iptables -t nat -D POSTROUTING -o ens2 -j MASQUERADE

SERVER
wg set wg0 peer <client_pubkey> allowed-ips 10.0.0.x/32

wg set wg0 peer ZiCIkzOxtTKduQpaXqO4lcHgA/P9YCw/gy/+e571BVM= allowed-ips 10.0.0.6/32



curl -sfL https://get.k3s.io | K3S_URL=https://10.0.0.1:6443 K3S_TOKEN=K1047421038c1a6b28861b8d4ac1e001f0ce65c8201606732dc444c47c837c3864f::server:ca4392a1e9d257daeaf4ab0d57237226  sh -

# nano /etc/systemd/system/k3s-agent.service
[Service]
ExecStart=
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/k3s agent --node-ip 10.0.0.x --flannel-iface=wg0

Problems:
if the host restart the vpn interface has to be up again (Solved)
We need to assing ip to each peer
Send the public ip of each peer to centralize server
Nodes can't have the same name

Add a new peer:
wg set wg0 peer "K30I8eIxuBL3OA43Xl34x0Tc60wqyDBx4msVm8VLkAE=" allowed-ips 10.101.1.2/32
ip -4 route add 10.101.1.2/32 dev wg0
Remove a peer:
wg set wg0 peer "K30I8eIxuBL3OA43Xl34x0Tc60wqyDBx4msVm8VLkAE=" remove
ip -4 route delete 10.101.1.2/32 dev wg0

# save to config
wg-quick save wg0

ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/k3s server --advertise-address 10.0.0.1 --flannel-iface=wg0
sudo cat /var/lib/rancher/k3s/server/node-token
